attributes.description: Recommended tools, libraries, and learning resources for further exploration of permission systems.

post.html:
Resources & Next Steps

Continue your authorization journey with these resources.

Libraries

CASL

  • Website: https://casl.js.org
  • Best for: JavaScript/TypeScript apps, isomorphic code
  • Highlights: No DSL, strong TypeScript, React/Vue integrations

Casbin

  • Website: https://casbin.org
  • Best for: Multi-language systems, complex RBAC
  • Highlights: DSL-based, policy adapters, many languages

Oso

  • Website: https://osohq.com
  • Best for: Embedded authorization engine
  • Highlights: Polar language, batteries included

ReBAC / Zanzibar-style

SpiceDB (by AuthZed)

  • Website: https://authzed.com
  • Best for: Zanzibar-style ReBAC at scale
  • Open source: Yes

OpenFGA (by Auth0/Okta)

  • Website: https://openfga.dev
  • Best for: Relationship-based access control
  • Open source: Yes

Managed Services

Permit.io

  • Full-featured authorization as a service

Cerbos

  • Open-source, self-hosted policy engine

WorkOS FGA

  • Fine-grained authorization API

Learning

OWASP

  • Access Control Cheat Sheet
  • Authorization Testing Guide

Papers

  • Google Zanzibar Paper - The foundational ReBAC paper

What We Built

In this workshop, you learned:

  • ✅ RBAC: Role-based route and action protection
  • ✅ ABAC: Ownership, org scoping, field-level permissions
  • ✅ ReBAC: Document sharing and relationship resolution
  • ✅ Libraries: CASL deep dive, Casbin overview
  • ✅ Custom engine: Built your own permission library
  • ✅ Integration: Frontend components, backend middleware
  • ✅ Production: Caching, auditing, testing

You now have the knowledge to implement robust authorization in any application.

Thank you for joining this workshop!
post.markdown:
# Resources & Next Steps

Continue your authorization journey with these resources.

## Libraries

### CASL

- **Website:** https://casl.js.org
- **Best for:** JavaScript/TypeScript apps, isomorphic code
- **Highlights:** No DSL, strong TypeScript, React/Vue integrations

### Casbin

- **Website:** https://casbin.org
- **Best for:** Multi-language systems, complex RBAC
- **Highlights:** DSL-based, policy adapters, many languages

### Oso

- **Website:** https://osohq.com
- **Best for:** Embedded authorization engine
- **Highlights:** Polar language, batteries included

## ReBAC / Zanzibar-style

### SpiceDB (by AuthZed)

- **Website:** https://authzed.com
- **Best for:** Zanzibar-style ReBAC at scale
- **Open source:** Yes

### OpenFGA (by Auth0/Okta)

- **Website:** https://openfga.dev
- **Best for:** Relationship-based access control
- **Open source:** Yes

## Managed Services

### Permit.io

- Full-featured authorization as a service

### Cerbos

- Open-source, self-hosted policy engine

### WorkOS FGA

- Fine-grained authorization API

## Learning

### OWASP

- [Access Control Cheat Sheet](https://cheatsheetseries.owasp.org/cheatsheets/Access_Control_Cheat_Sheet.html)
- [Authorization Testing Guide](https://owasp.org/www-project-web-security-testing-guide/)

### Papers

- [Google Zanzibar Paper](https://research.google/pubs/pub48190/) - The foundational ReBAC paper

## What We Built

In this workshop, you learned:

- ✅ RBAC: Role-based route and action protection
- ✅ ABAC: Ownership, org scoping, field-level permissions
- ✅ ReBAC: Document sharing and relationship resolution
- ✅ Libraries: CASL deep dive, Casbin overview
- ✅ Custom engine: Built your own permission library
- ✅ Integration: Frontend components, backend middleware
- ✅ Production: Caching, auditing, testing

You now have the knowledge to implement robust authorization in any application.

**Thank you for joining this workshop!**
post.title: Resources
